description: Additional binaries required for a survivable system under enforcement. These were missing from the initial discovery and must be resolved on the target machine.

power_management.reason: Without these, you cannot cleanly shut down or reboot under enforcement. You'd have to hard-power-off, risking filesystem corruption.

dbus.reason: D-Bus is the inter-process communication backbone. Without it, systemd services cannot talk to each other, NetworkManager cannot start, sudo may fail, and polkit auth breaks. The system becomes a zombie.

polkit.reason: PolicyKit handles privilege escalation authorization. Without it, GUI-based auth prompts fail, NetworkManager cannot be controlled by non-root users, and many systemd operations break.

networking_daemons.reason: Without network daemons in the map, WiFi and DHCP die after enforcement loads. You lose internet, which means you lose the ability to install anything or reach repos. This is what separates a fortress from a brick.

systemd_services.reason: These systemd helper binaries run as separate processes. If they're not in the map, their services fail at startup, causing cascading degradation.

ssh.reason: If you ever need remote access to recover the machine, sshd must be in the map. Without it, SSH dies on enforcement.

ai_stack.reason: The entire purpose of the system is to run governed AI. Ollama and its dependencies must be mapped. Also python3 scripts that Bombadil and the seeder run.
ai_stack.note: Ollama may be installed in a non-standard location. Use 'which ollama' to find it. Also check if Ollama spawns child processes — those need mapping too.

cron.reason: If you want scheduled tasks (log rotation, periodic attestation checks, etc.), cron must be mapped.

apt_infrastructure.reason: apt-get itself is mapped, but it spawns helper processes for HTTP, HTTPS, GPG verification, and dependency solving. Without these, package installation silently fails.

user_session.reason: If you use a desktop environment or even just a terminal emulator, these need to be mapped or the GUI session won't start.
user_session.note: These vary heavily by desktop environment. Run 'ps aux | grep -v kernel' under normal operation to see what processes are running, then map them all.

pam_and_auth.reason: PAM modules handle authentication. Some are separate executables that the LSM will block if not mapped.
pam_and_auth.note: PAM .so modules are loaded by dlopen(), not exec(), so the LSM may not block them. But unix_chkpwd IS executed as a separate process for password checks.

discovery_commands.description: Run these on your live system BEFORE enforcement to discover what needs mapping
discovery_commands.find_systemd_binaries: systemctl list-units --type=service --state=running --no-legend | awk '{print $1}' | xargs -I{} systemctl show {} -p ExecStart --value | grep -oP '/[^\s;]+' | sort -u
